answer:
There are two distinct concepts related to Consensus and the Model Context Protocol (MCP):

Consensus AI Connect offers an MCP server that acts as a secure bridge between AI platforms and Consensus workspaces. It uses OAuth2 for authentication and supports both local and remote deployment [1].

Separately, the consensus-tools organization provides an MCP server package (@consensus-tools/mcp) designed to expose agent workflows, job management, and human-in-the-loop approvals to MCP clients like Claude Code [2][3].

Additionally, there are unrelated projects, such as the Ephor Collaboration MCP Server, which focus on multi-agent debate and consensus-based decision-making [4].

results.title: Consensus AI Connect - MCP Server Security Information
Ephor Collaboration MCP Server - An integrated tool supporting multi-AI agent collaborative debates and realizing consensus decision-making
